mechanism_narrative: Persistent employment growth signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with rising PAYEMS, or the mean forward return is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy compresses valuation multiples and raises equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.0%, or the negative return premium is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in industrial production and retail sales indicates coordinated demand-supply expansion that amplifies earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when both INDPRO and RSAFS are jointly rising, or the interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation reduces real earnings expectations and tightens financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns in late-cycle environments.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of late-cycle months with rising CPIAUCSL, or the negative mean return fails to achieve statistical significance (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1250 thousand units signal weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 50% of weeks when HOUST is below 1250, or the negative mean 5-day return is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving capacity utilization and operating leverage, translating into positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of weeks with rising INDPRO, or mean 5-day return is negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment combined with elevated retail sales indicates a consumer strength feedback loop that drives above-trend equity returns through revenue surprises.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.0% and RSAFS exceeds 500000, or the joint signal's Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP exceeding 18500 billion chained 2017 dollars confirms aggregate demand durability, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 exceeds 18500, or the positive mean return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).
